title: Analysis capability registry entry
description: One self-describing entry per registered analysis. The popstats / atlas server loads the whole registry (an array of these) at startup; for each incoming request it looks up the matching analysis_type and decides whether to run live, hit a cache, or read from precompute, using `execution_modes` + `default_policy` modulated by the cost-based classifier (see cost-based_usage_data-management-policy-prompt.md).

analysis_type.description: Stable id of the analysis (e.g. 'fst_profile', 'pi_NS', 'iHS_window'). Used as the discriminator when routing requests.

module.description: Owning module / engine binary (e.g. 'popstats', 'codon_stats', 'fdM'). Multiple analyses can belong to one module.

provides.description: Result-schema version tags this analysis emits, e.g. ['fst_profile_v1', 'fst_profile_v2']. Clients select by version.

requires.description: Logical inputs this analysis needs. Free-form tokens; suggested vocabulary: 'beagle_gl', 'sample_list', 'groups', 'karyotype', 'interval', 'candidates_bed', 'windows_tsv', 'cache', 'genome_fa', 'gff', etc. Used by the server for pre-flight validation and for matching against what the request supplies.

execution_modes.description: Which storage tiers this analysis is even *capable* of. True means the engine supports the mode; false means it does not. The classifier may still narrow this further.

default_policy.description: Policy applied when the cost-based classifier has no opinion (e.g. unknown request shape). Must be one of the keys in `execution_modes` set to true.

allowed_policies.description: Optional explicit allow-list. If absent, derived from `execution_modes` (= the keys set to true). Useful when the engine can technically do a mode but you want to forbid it for this deployment (e.g. policy says 'no live mode for >1Mb intervals').

cost_class.description: Default cost class for typical-shape requests. The classifier may override per request shape (see cost-based_usage_data-management-policy-prompt.md).

reuse_scope.description: Free-form tag describing the natural unit of reuse. Suggested values: 'candidate' (cached per candidate id), 'chromosome' (per chrom), 'cohort' (per sample-set hash), 'genome' (one entry shared by all users), 'session' (per UI session), 'ephemeral' (per request, never reused).

result_schema.description: Output JSON schema id (e.g. 'fst_result_v1'). Should match one of the entries in `provides` and correspond to a schema file the server can validate against.

entrypoint.description: How the server invokes this analysis. For engine-binary entrypoints this is the binary name (e.g. 'popstats_fst'); for dispatcher-routed analyses it's the statistic discriminator (e.g. 'outlier_scan' — handed to popstats_dispatch via the activator's `.statistic` field).

engine_binding_ref.description: Optional. Path or $id of the activator schema whose `engine_binding` block describes the CLI flag mapping. Lets the dispatcher build argv off the schema instead of hardcoding.

deprecated.description: When true, the server should warn callers that the analysis is being phased out.

notes.description: Free-form notes for humans reading the registry.

consistency_rules.default_in_modes: default_policy MUST correspond to an execution_modes entry set to true.
consistency_rules.allowed_in_modes: Every value in allowed_policies (if present) MUST correspond to an execution_modes entry set to true.
consistency_rules.provides_has_schema: result_schema SHOULD appear in `provides` (canonical version) OR be a strict superset version.
